source: user request 2026-05-08 — 'every week we achive real deal with real build real debugging... that keeps on completing and debugging all tickets and stuff for itself'
title: scripts/sprint.py — autonomous ticket runner (Claude-driven, gated)
what_failed: No mechanism existed for Pi to autonomously progress through the open ticket queue. Every ticket required Ash to drive plan → edit → test → close manually. The 'continuous engineering loop' goal was aspirational only.
where_failed: Repo had no sprint runner; tickets sat open between sessions.
why_likely: Earlier phases (1–6) focused on per-ticket fixes and infrastructure; the meta-layer that picks and processes tickets was the natural next phase.
suggested_fix: Build scripts/sprint.py with: (a) ticket selection by severity, (b) Claude-driven plan generation, (c) optional auto-implement via Anthropic tool-use loop with read_file/write_file/edit_file/run_bash tools, (d) hard gates: RISK_FLAGGED components escalate immediately, only SAFE_COMPONENTS auto-implement, (e) cost cap (default $0.50) and ticket cap (default 1) and 15-min per-ticket timeout, (f) verify.py must PASS, with 1 retry, (g) on success: append SOLUTIONS, move ticket open→closed, refresh_pi, commit on a NEW branch sprint/T-NNN-slug — NEVER push, (h) escalation via Telegram if available.
fix_applied: Wrote scripts/sprint.py (~470 lines) implementing all the above. Risk-flagged list: pi_agent.py, agent/tools.py, agent/prompt.py, prompts/consciousness.txt, app/config.py, requirements.txt, memory/, .env. Safe components: scripts/, testing/, docs/, vault/. Tools sandboxed via _safe_path (refuses paths escaping ROOT) and _exec_tool (blocks risk-flagged paths and destructive bash). Default mode is plan-only — auto-implement requires --auto-implement flag.
tests: testing/test_sprint_runner.py — 24 passing tests covering risk classification, ticket selection, safe-path resolution, tool execution (read/write/edit/bash), close_ticket, plan generation (mocked), and full run_one_ticket escalation paths.
